title: v2 browser continuation interaction-assertion claims
description: Claim set of the RS256-signed JWT the host mints to hand a browser off to a plugin's launch_url continuation (see docs/security.md). The plugin's own origin verifies the signature against the host's published JWKS and exchanges the assertion for a session at POST /auth/contenttree/exchange (see openapi/plugin-api.yaml). This document describes the JWT *claim set* only (the decoded payload), not the compact JWS envelope itself.

iss.description: Issuer (RFC 7519 'iss'): the host's stable identifier, typically its HTTPS origin. The plugin MUST verify this matches the host it registered/trusts.

aud.description: Audience (RFC 7519 'aud'): the intended recipient, i.e. the plugin_id (or the plugin's HTTPS origin) this assertion was minted for. The plugin MUST reject assertions whose 'aud' does not identify itself.

sub.description: Subject (RFC 7519 'sub'): opaque, host-assigned identifier for the end user on whose behalf the browser continuation is happening. Domain-neutral: this repository imposes no further meaning on the identifier.

exp.description: Expiration time (RFC 7519 'exp'). MUST be enforced by the verifier; see docs/security.md for recommended short lifetimes for browser continuation assertions.

jti.description: JWT ID (RFC 7519 'jti'). MUST be treated as single-use: the verifying plugin rejects any assertion whose 'jti' it has already consumed, even if 'exp' has not yet elapsed.

invocation_id.description: The invoke() call this browser continuation belongs to, binding the assertion to a specific deferred operation's launch_url handoff.

tree_id.description: The content tree this browser continuation is scoped to.
